## Data

<!-- TODO: name the real public source (BCB SGS, IBGE SIDRA, FRED, Eurostat, OECD) and
     pull it via API instead of the synthetic series below. -->

For now, a synthetic quarterly series stands in for the real data pull:


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
set.seed(42)
n <- 80
series <- arima.sim(model = list(ar = 0.6), n = n) |> as.numeric()
dates <- seq.Date(from = as.Date("2006-01-01"), by = "quarter", length.out = n)
df <- data.frame(date = dates, y = series)
tail(df)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
         date       y
75 2024-07-01 -0.3268
76 2024-10-01  0.7373
77 2025-01-01  1.2641
78 2025-04-01  2.1506
79 2025-07-01  0.8142
80 2025-10-01  1.1389
```


:::
:::


## Method

<!-- TODO: state the actual model/method in LaTeX. Example below: a simple AR(1). -->

A simple AR(1) forecasting model:

$$
y_t = \phi \, y_{t-1} + \varepsilon_t, \qquad \varepsilon_t \sim \mathcal{N}(0, \sigma^2)
$$

## Code


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
fit <- arima(df$y, order = c(1, 0, 0))
fc <- predict(fit, n.ahead = 4)

fc_df <- data.frame(
  date = seq.Date(from = max(df$date), by = "quarter", length.out = 5)[-1],
  y = as.numeric(fc$pred),
  lower = as.numeric(fc$pred - 1.96 * fc$se),
  upper = as.numeric(fc$pred + 1.96 * fc$se)
)

fit
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```

Call:
arima(x = df$y, order = c(1, 0, 0))

Coefficients:
        ar1  intercept
      0.596     -0.043
s.e.  0.089      0.275

sigma^2 estimated as 1.02:  log likelihood = -114.5,  aic = 235
```


:::
:::


## Figure


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(df, aes(date, y)) +
  geom_line(color = "#0f766e") +
  geom_ribbon(data = fc_df, aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), alpha = 0.15, fill = "#14b8a6") +
  geom_line(data = fc_df, color = "#14b8a6", linewidth = 1) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
```
